name: ForSeeBench
description: Evaluation benchmark for prospective audio-description question answering. Public rows contain prior AD context, a question, and answer options. Answer-bearing rows add scoring labels and clean audit metadata.
citation: @misc{forseebench2026, title = {ForSeeBench: Prospective Audio-Description QA for Evaluating Accessible Movie Understanding}, author = {Anonymous Author(s)}, year = {2026}, note = {Anonymous benchmark submission artifact}}
rai:dataLimitations: ForSeeBench is an evaluation benchmark, not a training corpus.
The benchmark is derived from movie audio-description data and should not be interpreted as complete accessibility evaluation.
The release excludes raw movie videos, clips, audio tracks, subtitles, dialogue files, full MAD/MAD-eval assets, and restricted source assets.
rai:dataBiases: The source domain is movie audio description and may reflect movie-domain, genre, casting, and description-style biases.
Automated Qwen-assisted filtering may favor examples with explicit textual evidence.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: Derived text may contain fictional character names and movie scene descriptions.
The artifact is intended for benchmark review and should not be treated as a source of personal or biometric information.
rai:dataUseCases: Intended use: evaluating multiple-choice prospective QA from prior AD context.
Intended use: comparing AD context sources and context policies under fixed Q/A items.
Out-of-scope use: training on restricted source assets, raw media redistribution, accessibility certification, or high-stakes deployment.
rai:dataSocialImpact: ForSeeBench supports evaluation of forward-relevant information in AD streams. Risks include overclaiming accessibility impact or using prospective QA accuracy as a substitute for blind/low-vision user studies.
rai:hasSyntheticData: Yes. Questions, answer options, distractors, and some validation metadata are Qwen-assisted; prior context and hidden target AD text are derived from MAD/MAD-eval audio-description data.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: MAD / MAD-eval
prov:wasDerivedFrom.citation: Soldan et al., MAD: A Scalable Dataset for Language Grounding in Videos From Movie Audio Descriptions, CVPR 2022
prov:wasDerivedFrom.license: Original MAD/MAD-eval and underlying movie source assets remain governed by their original providers' terms and are not redistributed in this release.

recordSet.name: public_examples
recordSet.description: Public no-answer prospective QA examples for model prediction.

field.name: prior_context
field.description: List of prior AD context objects.
